title: G-PPG: A Gesture-related PPG-based Two-Factor Authentication for Wearable Devices
venue: 2022 IEEE 28th International Conference on Parallel and Distributed Systems (ICPADS)

method.input_representation: Combination of 8 specific gesture-related PPG features and 20 general signal features (time, frequency, and time-frequency domains)
method.training_strategy: Binary classification of legitimate user vs attackers with an adaptive update mechanism for long-term stability

evaluation_protocol: Leave-one-user-out (one user as legal, others as attackers), randomized gesture selection

reported_limitations: Lower recall during walking scenarios compared to sitting

usability.usability_issues: Enrollment overhead may reduce user experience

key_contribution: Proposed G-PPG utilizing gesture-induced changes in PPG signals for authentication
Customized feature set combining wearing habits and physiological characteristics
Adaptive update mechanism to handle temporal changes in user habits
main_strength: Long-term stability through periodic model updates
main_weakness: Lack of sophisticated presentation attacks/spoofing tests
insights.notes_for_review: The paper focuses on 'Gesture-related PPG' as a way to mitigate motion artifacts which typically plague wrist-PPG authentication.
